us-politics.headline: Iran Tensions Rise as Graham Warns of Global Blockade
us-politics.body:
Senator Lindsey Graham escalated rhetoric around Iran, suggesting a potential global blockade could emerge soon. The comments come as tensions in the Strait of Hormuz continue to simmer, with Republican lawmakers divided on supporting potential military action.

Congressional Republicans show internal fractures over presidential war powers. Some members want to check potential military escalation, while others support a more aggressive stance toward Iran. The debate intensifies as the 60-day mark for potential military engagement approaches.

sources.title: Lindsey Graham says blockade against Iran 'could become global soon'
Republicans divided on whether to check Trump’s Iran war power as 60-day mark looms

us-politics.headline: GOP Budget Battle Reveals Deep Party Divisions
us-politics.body:
Senate Republicans pushed through a budget resolution in a late-night voting marathon, but not without significant internal disagreement. Two GOP senators - Murkowski and Paul - broke ranks on the final vote, highlighting growing ideological splits.

The budget process saw multiple amendments challenged, with some Republican senators backing measures on drug pricing and health care costs that leadership opposed. These fractures suggest deeper tensions within the party as midterm elections approach.

sources.title: Two GOP senators break ranks on final Senate budget vote
Senate GOP rams through blueprint to bankroll ICE, Border Patrol through end of Trump era
Senate Republicans adopt budget resolution in late-night voting marathon
Three GOP senators back Sanders amendment to slash drug prices
Four GOP senators vote against adding Save America Act to budget package

us-politics.headline: Trump's Polling Numbers Tumble as 2026 Midterms Loom
us-politics.body:
President Trump faces mounting political challenges as his popularity continues to decline. Recent polling indicates an uphill battle for both the president and Republican candidates in upcoming elections.

Democrats have shown strong fundraising capabilities, though Republicans maintain a significant cash advantage. The political landscape remains volatile, with internal party debates and shifting voter sentiments creating uncertainty about the midterm outcomes.

sources.title: Trump facing an uphill battle with voters as polling numbers continue to tumble
President Trump's popularity is falling. What does it means for him and the GOP?
Democrats dominate midterm fundraising, but Republicans have a huge cash advantage
GOP tries to ease growing fears of midterm shellacking

au-politics.headline: NDIS Reforms Spark Disability Support Debate
au-politics.body:
The federal government's proposed changes to the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) have ignited fierce controversy. Proposed eligibility assessments could dramatically reduce support for people with disabilities, with Canberra residents expressing deep concerns about potential cuts to vital services.

Experts warn the new assessment tool might inadvertently exclude vulnerable participants. The government argues the changes will improve scheme sustainability, but disability advocates see them as a direct threat to essential support networks.

sources.title: Canberrans with disability fear NDIS changes will cut vital support
How the new eligibility test for NDIS participants could work
Rinehart’s $200m donation to convert homes for veterans welcomed by RSL – as it happened

au-politics.headline: Political Tensions Rise Over Gas Taxation and Energy Policy
au-politics.body:
Prime Minister Anthony Albanese appears poised to block proposed tax increases on gas companies, signaling potential friction within government energy policy circles. The move comes amid complex negotiations about resource taxation and national economic strategy.

The decision could have significant implications for Australia's energy sector and broader economic planning. Political observers are watching closely as the government navigates competing interests between resource companies and public revenue needs.

sources.title: Albanese poised to kill off move to increase taxes on gas giants
Canavan's frosty foray into Farrer's campaign trail

au-politics.headline: AFL Player Wins Appeal in Controversial Conduct Case
au-politics.body:
St Kilda footballer Lance Collard has successfully reduced a ban originally imposed for a homophobic slur, raising questions about disciplinary standards in professional sports. The appeal process highlighted ongoing tensions around appropriate conduct and consequences.

The case has reignited discussions about player behavior, institutional responses to discriminatory language, and the balance between punishment and rehabilitation in professional athletic environments.

sources.title: Collard's 'crippling' ban for homophobic slur reduced on appeal
The Green party candidate who wants to ‘burn Zionism to the ground’

world.headline: Israel Escalates Lebanon Conflict - Kills Journalist, Razes Villages
world.body:
Israeli forces have intensified their assault on Lebanon, killing journalist Amal Khalil in a targeted strike and systematically levelling villages behind the so-called yellow line. The attack on Khalil marks a significant escalation in tensions - one that threatens to broaden the regional conflict beyond Gaza.

The strike drew immediate condemnation from media organizations and Lebanese officials, who accused Israel of deliberately targeting journalists. Meanwhile, Israeli military operations continue to demolish Lebanese border villages, raising fears of a wider confrontation.

sources.title: What we know about Israel killing Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil
Israeli forces levelling Lebanese villages behind ‘yellow line’
Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
Israel kills journalist and wounds another in south Lebanon targeted attack

world.headline: US Navy Leadership Collapses After Sudden Political Upheaval
world.body:
The US Navy's top leadership has been abruptly dismantled after Navy Secretary John Phelan was fired by conservative media personality Pete Hegseth. The Pentagon confirmed Phelan's immediate departure - a move that signals deep political interference in military leadership.

Hegseth - a vocal right-wing commentator with close ties to former political circles - has now directly influenced senior military appointments. The sudden leadership change raises questions about the stability of military institutions and potential political motivations behind the dismissal.

sources.title: Who is John Phelan the US Navy Secretary fired by Pete Hegseth?
US Navy chief leaving post 'effective immediately', Pentagon says

world.headline: Iran War Enters Complex Phase as Regional Tensions Simmer
world.body:
The ongoing conflict with Iran has reached a critical juncture - with diplomatic talks stalled and military tensions rising. President Trump's recent ceasefire extension has done little to calm the situation, with Iran seizing a ship in the Strait of Hormuz and continuing strategic maneuvers.

Protesters in London have taken to the streets against the war, highlighting international divisions. The conflict continues to reshape geopolitical dynamics, with potential spillover effects across the Middle East.

sources.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 55 after Trump extended ceasefire?
Protesters stabbed at London rally against war on Iran
Iran releases video of IRGC seizing ship in the Strait of Hormuz
Iran war live: Israel kills Lebanese journalist; Tehran-US talks stalled

ai.headline: AI Reshapes Newsrooms and Workplace Productivity
ai.body:
Major tech companies are rapidly integrating AI into workplace tools and editorial processes. Google has updated Workspace to position AI as a virtual office intern - promising to automate routine tasks and boost productivity. Meanwhile, newsrooms are developing explicit AI policies to navigate ethical challenges around content generation and attribution.

OpenAI is pushing further, launching tools that allow teams to create custom bots capable of performing independent work tasks. These developments signal a transformative moment for knowledge work - with AI moving from experimental technology to core operational infrastructure.

sources.title: Google updates Workspace to make AI your new office intern
Our newsroom AI policy
OpenAI now lets teams make custom bots that can do work on their own

ai.headline: Tech Giants Battle for AI Chip Supremacy
ai.body:
The AI hardware race is heating up as Google Cloud launches two new AI chips directly challenging Nvidia's market dominance. This strategic move underscores the critical importance of specialized computing infrastructure for advanced machine learning models.

The chip development comes amid growing concerns about AI's broader economic impact. Senator Elizabeth Warren has warned that potential AI failures could trigger the next financial crisis - highlighting the high stakes of technological innovation and systemic risk.

sources.title: Google Cloud launches two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia
AI failure could trigger the next financial crisis, warns Elizabeth Warren
OpenAI's response to the Axios developer tool compromise

btc.headline: Bitcoin Wavers Near $80,000 as Market Sentiment Shifts
btc.body: Bitcoin's price momentum stalled Thursday, with the cryptocurrency struggling to maintain its recent gains near the $80,000 mark. Traders saw profit-taking pressure emerge, causing a slight pullback in the market. Technical indicators suggest a delicate balance between bullish sentiment and potential correction.

sources.title: Bitcoin's bull score index just left bear territory. There's a warning attached.
Bitcoin loses grip near $80,000 as ETH, SOL, DOGE fade on profit-taking

btc.headline: Pantera Capital Pushes Satsuma to Liquidate Bitcoin Holdings
btc.body: Investment firm Pantera Capital has urged Satsuma to sell its Bitcoin holdings as the company's shares plummet 99%. The dramatic share price collapse highlights the volatile intersection of traditional finance and cryptocurrency investments. Pantera's intervention signals growing pressure on firms to manage their crypto assets strategically.

sources.title: The DAT collapse: Pantera wants Satsuma to dump its bitcoin as shares crash 99%

food.description: Spicy poached eggs nestled in a vibrant tomato and bell pepper sauce, topped with crumbled feta and fresh herbs
food.tip: Use cast iron skillet for even heat distribution and authentic crusty edges

health.name: Desk Warrior Reset
health.description: Perform a targeted 5-minute mobility sequence that counteracts prolonged sitting by opening chest, releasing hip flexors, and realigning spine
health.tip: Use a foam roller along spine to instantly decompress and reset thoracic alignment

learn.description: Emerging field where city infrastructure and architecture directly mimic natural ecosystem principles like self-regulation, efficiency, and adaptive resilience
learn.why: Offers revolutionary solutions to climate adaptation and sustainable urban living by studying how complex natural systems actually work
